hero.title: Multi-scale digital twins.
hero.description: A complete BIM, GIS, and open-data platform that runs in the browser — deployed your way, customized to your needs, free of vendor lock-in. Cost-recovery economics. Source available.
hero.videoFallback: Your browser does not support the video tag.

mission.title: Connecting people, places, and the data that shapes them.
mission.body: We're a non-profit foundation building shared infrastructure for digital twins — so institutions, communities, and researchers can collaborate on the buildings, neighbourhoods, and landscapes they share. The platform is the means. Connection is the point.

demos.title: See the platform in action.
demos.subtitle: Short walk-throughs of the platform in action — from the general overview to focused tools built for specific use cases.

general.title: Platform overview
general.body: A guided tour of the digital twin workspace — bringing BIM, GIS, and open data together in a single browser session, with the tools institutions actually use day-to-day.

viewers.title: Platform viewers
viewers.body: Map, BIM, and point-cloud viewers backed by site, building, and file databases — searchable through buildingSMART Data Dictionary properties. IFC, GeoJSON, point clouds, documents, and media live alongside each building, and the platform is extensible via <link>custom plugins</link>.

organizational.title: BIM + GIS integration
organizational.body: Building-scale BIM and infrastructure-scale GIS data load together in a single browser environment, with precise geolocation and support for multiple coordinate systems. Multi-gigabyte portfolios resolve in under two seconds per model, and users can drop geolocated comments and models onto the map to collaborate.

open-data.title: Open data federation
open-data.body: Federates dozens of open government datasets pulled through standardized APIs with no manual preparation, and users can connect their own open data sources from anywhere in the world. Layer neighbourhoods, transit, infrastructure, and more alongside your buildings, then tune colour scales and visualization quality for analysis.

openBIM.title: openBIM viewer
openBIM.body: Browser-native IFC processing that preserves the full schema — every attribute, material, constraint, and custom property set. Add DXF, GLTF, FBX, OBJ, or point-cloud overlays; section, measure, and comment on the geometry; track issues with BCF; validate models against IDS exchange requirements.

sensors.title: IoT sensors on the map and BIM
sensors.body: Add live IoT sensors directly onto the map and into the BIM model. Stream readings into the digital twin to connect physical infrastructure with its digital counterpart.

wildfires.title: Visualizing wildfires
wildfires.body: An interactive map built with a wildfire research group to monitor fire conditions across Canada — combining open datasets from the Canadian Interagency Forest Fire Centre (CIFFC) with photos and videos crowdsourced from users in near-real time.

bridge.title: Bridge BIM, GIS, and open data
bridge.body: Visualize and analyze multi-scale digital twins in a single browser-based workspace — from individual buildings to regional landscapes.

interop.title: Interoperable by design
interop.body: Open standards and open formats. Connect existing pipelines and tools without proprietary gatekeepers.

extend.title: Extend and integrate
extend.body: Build analysis tools, custom extensions, and integrations through documented APIs.

deploy.title: Deploy your way
deploy.body: Use the managed sovereign service or self-host the AGPL-3.0 core on your infrastructure. Switch between modes without re-platforming.

customize.title: Customize without forking
customize.body: Commission specialized features on a cost-recovery basis — built on the open core so you stay on the upgrade path.

train.title: Equip your team
train.body: Formal training and capacity-building programs for institutions adopting digital twin practice.

solutions.title: Pick your deployment.
solutions.subtitle: Four routes to value — every one priced on cost-recovery, with any surplus reinvested in the platform.

managedCloud.name: Managed Cloud (SaaS)
managedCloud.headline: Sovereign hosting, ready in days.
managedCloud.body: We run the platform on sovereign infrastructure of your choice, with included support, automatic updates, and data residency guarantees. Cost-recovery pricing — no profit margin.

selfManaged.name: Self-Managed Deployment
selfManaged.headline: Run the open-source core on your infrastructure.
selfManaged.body: Full AGPL-3.0 source, documentation, and community support. Keep complete control over your data, network, and integrations.

customDev.name: Custom Development
customDev.headline: Specialized tools, built on the open core.
customDev.body: We co-develop specialized analysis tools and platform extensions on a cost-recovery basis. Surplus reinvested. Where the work benefits the commons, it's contributed upstream.

training.name: Training & Enablement
training.headline: Equip your team to operate and extend.
training.body: Workshops, documentation, and capacity-building programs for institutions adopting digital twin practice. Tailored to your team's role and proficiency.

whyCdt.title: Built to remove the things that usually go wrong.

noLockIn.title: No vendor lock-in
noLockIn.body: AGPL-3.0 source means you can always self-host, fork, or exit. Your investment in the platform is portable.

dataSovereignty.title: Data sovereignty
dataSovereignty.body: Sovereign managed hosting in the region of your choice, or fully on-premise self-hosting. You decide where your data lives.

costRecovery.title: Cost-recovery economics
costRecovery.body: We're a non-profit. No profit margin baked in. Any surplus is reinvested in the platform — not extracted.

multidisciplinary.title: Multidisciplinary by design
multidisciplinary.body: Built from the start to connect BIM, GIS, research, and operations. The platform fits the way institutions actually work.

openStandards.title: Open Standards
openStandards.description: Eliminate proprietary barriers and promote interoperability through open standards.

communityDriven.title: Community Driven
communityDriven.description: Build with and for the community.

transparent.title: Transparent
transparent.description: Foster trust and collaborative development through open source code.

imagination.title: Imagination
imagination.description: Challenge current boundaries that limit what is possible with open source digital technologies.

multidisciplinary.title: Multidisciplinary
multidisciplinary.description: Where we are today is evidence that we are stronger and smarter together.

accessible.title: Accessible
accessible.description: Empower everyone with intuitive, user-friendly tools that work directly in the browser.

developers.title: Extend the platform. Integrate your systems.
developers.body: Documented APIs and a contributor ecosystem for analysis tools, extensions, and integrations. Build alongside the core — or on top of it.

leadership.title: Board-governed. Mission-bound.
leadership.intro: Directed by a board with deep expertise across architecture, technology, research, and education. As a non-profit, our charter requires that the platform serves users — not shareholders.

contact.title: See it for yourself.
contact.description: Tell us about your team and use case. We'll set up a tailored walk-through and outline a deployment path that fits.

footer.description: Multi-scale digital twin infrastructure for institutions. Open standards, cost-recovery pricing, no vendor lock-in.
footer.governance: Canadian non-profit corporation. Board-governed. Source AGPL-3.0. Surplus reinvested in the platform and the community.
footer.stewardship: Stewarded by a Canadian not-for-profit organization for long-term public benefit
footer.copyright: Collab Digital Twins. All rights reserved.
